description: Reference mapping patterns learned once from supplied RE sample output workbooks. Runtime application does not require sample outputs.

rules.notes: The value is mapped using the Translation table (Column A = Original Value, Column B = Include/Exclude). If no match is found, the output displays “-”.

rules.source_column: DATA.FHA_CASE_#, INVESTOR-JAN.FHA CASE #, INVESTOR-JAN.MSR HOLDER (CLIENT NAME)
rules.notes: Return the MSR Holder (Client Name) from the Investor-Jan sheet for the matching FHA Case #. If no matching FHA Case # exists, leave the result blank.

Allowed values for DOCUMENT_PACKAGE_ITEM_DESCRIPTION:
Annual Occupancy Certification
Bankruptcy - Chapter 13
Conservator
Death Certificate
Evidence of Completion of Required Repairs
Flood Insurance
Hardest Hit Funds
Hazard Insurance
HOA/Condo Paid Receipt
Life Estate
Loss Draft Evidence
Payment Plan Document
POA
Prior Servicer - Loan Balance Transactions
Property Tax Statement
Tax Deferral Program
Trust
Wind Insurance
Readable format: COALESCE(llsr.column_b_value, '') AS <column_name>

Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN st.mca >= 0.97 AND st.total_imp_count <= 3
    THEN 'PRIORITY 1'

    WHEN st.mca >= 0.97 AND st.total_imp_count <= 6
    THEN 'PRIORITY 2'

    WHEN st.mca >= 0.97 AND st.total_imp_count <= 9
    THEN 'PRIORITY 3'

    ELSE 'PRIORITY 4'
END AS priority_level

Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN llsr.loan_status = 'In Process'
    THEN 'In Process'
    ELSE ''
END AS loan_status_filtered

rules.transformation_logic: =IF(OR(VLOOKUP(Imp.LOAN_SKEY[row],'Loan Level List'!B:I,8,FALSE)="Mortgage Assets Management,LLC",VLOOKUP(Imp.LOAN_SKEY[row],'Loan Level List'!B:I,8,FALSE)="Waterfall Asset Management,LLC"),"Y","N")
rules.source_object: Same excel workbook(Imp, Loan Level List)
rules.notes:
CASE
    WHEN lll.investor_name IN (
        'Mortgage Assets Management, LLC',
        'Waterfall Asset Management, LLC'
    )
    THEN 'Y'
    ELSE 'N'
END AS investor_flag
``

CASE
    WHEN st.mca < 0.78 THEN '<78%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.79 THEN '78-79%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.80 THEN '79-80%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.81 THEN '80-81%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.82 THEN '81-82%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.83 THEN '82-83%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.84 THEN '83-84%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.85 THEN '84-85%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.86 THEN '85-86%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.87 THEN '86-87%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.88 THEN '87-88%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.89 THEN '88-89%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.90 THEN '89-90%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.91 THEN '90-91%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.92 THEN '91-92%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.93 THEN '92-93%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.94 THEN '93-94%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.95 THEN '94-95%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.96 THEN '95-96%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.97 THEN '96-97%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.98 THEN '97-98%'
    WHEN st.mca < 0.99 THEN '98-99%'
    WHEN st.mca < 1.00 THEN '99-100%'
    WHEN st.mca < 1.05 THEN '100-105%'
    WHEN st.mca < 1.10 THEN '105-110%'
    ELSE '>110%'
END AS mca_bucket
``

Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN st.loan_status = 'In Process'
    THEN llsr.col_66_value
    ELSE ''
END AS <target_column>

rules.source_column: SUPPLEMENTAL FILED DEC 22- CURR.INITIAL_APPROVED, SUPPLEMENTAL FILED DEC 22- CURR.COMPLETE_DATE
rules.notes: Calculate the number of days between Initial_Approved and Complete_Date.

rules.transformation_logic:
CASE
        WHEN b.workflow_task_skey IN ('8010020', '8030020')
            THEN 'Initial_Claim'
        ELSE 'Supplemental_Claim'
    END AS department
FORMULA_TEXT: =IF(ISBLANK(INDEX(Data!G:G,MATCH(Supplemental Filed Dec 22- curr.FHA_Case_#,Data!B:B,0))),"",INDEX(Data!G:G,MATCH(Supplemental Filed Dec 22- curr.FHA_Case_#,Data!B:B,0)))
rules.source_column: SUPPLEMENTAL FILED DEC 22- CURR.FHA_CASE_#, DATA.FHA_CASE_#, DATA.SUPPLEMENTAL_DUE_DATE
rules.notes: Look up the FHA Case # from Supplemental Filed Dec 22- curr.FHA_Case_# in Data.FHA_CASE_#, and return the matching Data.SUPPLEMENTAL_DUE_DATE. If the returned value is blank, return blank.

rules.source_column: SUPPLEMENTAL FILED DEC 22- CURR.LOAN_SKEY, INVESTOR-JAN.LOAN SKEY, INVESTOR-JAN.MSR HOLDER (CLIENT NAME)
rules.notes: Look up loan_skey from Supplemental Filed Dec 22- curr.loan_skey in Investor-Jan.Loan Skey, and return the matching Investor-Jan.MSR Holder (Client Name).

rules.transformation_logic: FORMULA_TEXT: =IFNA(IF(OR([@MAM]="Mortgage Assets Management, LLC",[@MAM]="Waterfall Asset Management, LLC"),"MAM","Non-MAM"),"Non-MAM")
rules.notes: If MAM equals Mortgage Assets Management, LLC or Waterfall Asset Management, LLC, return MAM; otherwise return Non-MAM. If there is any error or missing value, return Non-MAM.

rules.source_column: SUPPLEMENTAL FILED DEC 22- CURR.INITIAL_APPROVED, SUPPLEMENTAL FILED DEC 22- CURR.COMPLETE_DATE
rules.notes: Calculate the number of working days between Initial_Approved and Complete_Date, excluding weekends.

rules.transformation_logic: Worksheets("Supplier Template").Range("ai2:ai" & Range("a" & Rows.Count).End(xlUp).Row).Formula = "=IF(AH2=""Current"",""Within SLA"",IF(AG2=""Incorrect Status/Sub Status Provided"",""Incorrect Status Provided"",""Outside of SLA""))"
rules.notes:
Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN st.sla_category = 'Current'
    THEN 'Within SLA'
    WHEN st.sla_category = 'Incorrect Status/Sub Status Provided'
    THEN 'Incorrect Status Provided'
    ELSE 'Outside of SLA'
END AS cadence_status

    WHEN st.supplier_status IN ('G7_value', 'G9_value', 'G19_value')
    THEN COALESCE(ir.date_sent_to_supplier, CURRENT_DATE)
    ELSE CURRENT_DATE
END AS date_sent_to_supplier
DATEDIFF(DAY, st.status_substatus_date, CURRENT_DATE) AS days_in_status

Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN (st.days_in_status - st.sla) < 1
    THEN 0
    ELSE (st.days_in_status - st.sla)
END AS days_over_sla
DATEDIFF(DAY, st.date_sent_to_supplier,CURRENT_DATE) AS days_since_referred

COALESCE(llsr.llsr_comments, '') AS llsr_comments

rules.transformation_logic: Worksheets("Supplier Template").Range("af2:af" & Range("a" & Rows.Count).End(xlUp).Row).Formula = "=IF(OR(O2='SLA Translation'!$G$19,O2='SLA Translation'!$G$9,O2='SLA Translation'!$G$7),VLOOKUP(AB2&AC2,'SLA Translation'!$D$3:$E$46,2,0))"
rules.notes:
Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN st.supplier_status IN ('G7_value', 'G9_value', 'G19_value')
    THEN sla.sla_days
END AS sla

rules.transformation_logic: Worksheets("Supplier Template").Range("ah2:ah" & Range("a" & Rows.Count).End(xlUp).Row).Formula = "=IFNA(IF(AG2=0,""Current"",IF(AG2<16,""1-15 Over SLA"",IF(AG2<31,""15-30 Over SLA"",IF(AG2<61,""30-60 Over SLA"",""60+ Days Over SLA"")))),""Incorrect Status/Sub Status Provided"")"
rules.notes:
Readable format:
CASE
    WHEN st.days_over_sla IS NULL
    THEN 'Incorrect Status/Sub Status Provided'
    WHEN st.days_over_sla = 0
    THEN 'Current'
    WHEN st.days_over_sla < 16
    THEN '1-15 Over SLA'
    WHEN st.days_over_sla < 31
    THEN '15-30 Over SLA'
    WHEN st.days_over_sla < 61
    THEN '30-60 Over SLA'
    ELSE '60+ Days Over SLA'
END AS sla_category

rules.transformation_logic: Worksheets("Supplier Template").Range("ad2:ad" & Range("a" & Rows.Count).End(xlUp).Row).Formula = "=IFNA(IF(OR(O2='SLA Translation'!$G$19,O2='SLA Translation'!$G$9,O2='SLA Translation'!$G$7),VLOOKUP(A2,'[2. Insurance.xlsb]Insurance Report'!$A:$AD,30,0),TODAY()),TODAY())"

``
If Qualified Date is “-”, return “-”; otherwise compare it with the reference date (5/17/2026). If it is later, return “< 1 Year”; otherwise return “> 1 Year”.

Take the LOAN_SKEY and search for it in the Audit Tracking sheet using Skey (column N).
If a match is found, return the corresponding Date (column P).
If no match is found, display “-”.

Take the LOAN_SKEY and search for it in the Audit Tracking sheet using Skey (column N).
If a match is found, return the corresponding Issue (column O).
If no match is found, display “-”.

Take the LOAN_SKEY and search for it in the Inactive Alerts sheet using loan_skey (column B).
If a match is found, return the corresponding alert_type_description (column D).
If no match is found, display “-”.

Take the LOAN_SKEY and search for it in the Inactive Alerts sheet using loan_skey (column B).
If a match is found, return the corresponding modified_date (column U).
If no match is found, display “-”.
Provide your feedback on BizChat

Take the LOAN_SKEY and search for it in the Inactive Servicer Cure sheet using loan_skey.
If a match is found, return the corresponding modified_date.
If no match is found, display “-”.
If both Inactive Alert Date and Audit Tracking Date are “-”, return “-”; otherwise return the most recent date among Inactive Servicer Cure Alert, Inactive Alert Date, and Audit Tracking Date.
Compare Inactive Alert Date and Audit Tracking Date. If the most recent date is Inactive Alert Date, return Inactive Alert; otherwise return Audit Tracking Issue.
If Most Recent Date is “-”, return “-”; otherwise add 365 days to the Most Recent Date and return the result (Qualified Date).
If Inactive Servicer Cure Alert is not “-”, and Age Bucket is not “-” and equals “< 1 Year”, and Validation Alert Status is “-”, then return “Qualified”; otherwise return “Not Qualified”.

Take the LOAN_SKEY and search for it in the Active Validation Alert sheet using loan_skey (column B). If a match is found, return the corresponding alert_type_description (column D); if no match is found, display “-”.
